---
title: "Diversity by dissemination area — Toronto"
description: "Visible-minority composition at Statistics Canada's finest standard geography, for the Toronto metro area (2021 Census)"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os, json
import pandas as pd
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_groups_map
```

This map drops below the census tract to the **dissemination area (DA)** — Statistics Canada's *smallest* standard geography, about **400–700 people each** (several DAs make up one tract). It's the level neighbourhood apps such as HouseSigma use, where each little area is labelled by its StatCan ID — the "Statistics Canada Area #…" you may have seen (for example area **#35200654**). This page covers the **Toronto** metro; a [Vancouver version](neighbourhoods.qmd) is also available. There are ~57,000 DAs nationally, so they're built a metro at a time to keep the map light.

Same descriptive framing as the tract map on the [main Population page](diversity.qmd#diversity-by-neighbourhood): "visible minority" is Statistics Canada's term, "Not a visible minority" includes white *and* Indigenous people, and there is no "good"/"bad" reading — it shows where people live. **Use the dropdown** to switch groups; zoom and pan to your street.

```{python}
#| echo: false
da_geo = "data/geo/da_toronto_2021.geojson"
da_path = "data/geo/statcan_da_toronto_ethnicity.csv"
if os.path.exists(da_geo) and os.path.exists(da_path):
    geo = json.load(open(da_geo))
    da = pd.read_csv(da_path, dtype={"dauid": str})
    groups = [("all_vm", "All visible minorities"), ("south_asian", "South Asian"),
              ("chinese", "Chinese"), ("black", "Black"), ("filipino", "Filipino"),
              ("arab", "Arab"), ("latin_american", "Latin American"),
              ("southeast_asian", "Southeast Asian"), ("west_asian", "West Asian"),
              ("korean", "Korean"), ("japanese", "Japanese"),
              ("not_vm", "Not a visible minority")]
    fig = choropleth_groups_map(geo, da, "dauid", groups, name_col="name",
        colorscale="Cividis", opacity=0.6, center={"lat": 43.72, "lon": -79.38}, zoom=8.5, height=720,
        source_note="Source: 2021 Census (Statistics Canada, Census Profile 98-401-X2021006); % of population by dissemination area, Toronto CMA. 'Visible minority' is StatCan's term; 'Not a visible minority' includes white and Indigenous people. Descriptive only.")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Toronto DA diversity map not available — run build_da_profile(cmauid='535', pruid='35', geono='006_Ontario', slug='toronto').")
```

*Dissemination areas with very small or suppressed populations are omitted. Download: [Toronto DA visible-minority shares (CSV)](../data/geo/statcan_da_toronto_ethnicity.csv) — 2021 Census (Statistics Canada).*

[← Back to Diversity](diversity.qmd)
